title: Bitdeer Convertible Notes and Dilution Concerns
text: Bitdeer Technologies announced a $300 million convertible notes offering in February 2026, raising concerns among investors about shareholder dilution. The company tumbled 17% on the debt raise announcement and concurrent stock sale. The convertible notes structure allows investors to convert debt into equity at prices above current levels, creating potential dilution when conversion occurs. Bitdeer's aggressive capital raise strategy reflects the company's substantial capital requirements for building major infrastructure projects including the Tydal AI data center in Norway (180 MW, $300+ million investment) and Rockdale power expansion in Texas. The company required this capital to fund simultaneous expansion in Bitcoin mining, AI cloud services, and hardware manufacturing. While dilution concerns are valid, the capital deployment supports long-term growth in higher-margin AI cloud services expected to generate $2 billion annually at scale. Investors weighed near-term dilution against long-term growth potential from AI infrastructure expansion.
